sourceMovie.title: Reagan
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Reagan works as a history movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Dallas Buyers Club
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Reagan clicked for you, Dallas Buyers Club (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Reagan?
faq.answer: Try Dallas Buyers Club, J. Edgar, A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood—each shares DNA with Reagan while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Reagan?
faq.answer: Reagan (2024) is best described as History, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Reagan worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
